title: Speaker verification
abstract: A device includes a memory, a receiver, a processor, and a display. The memory is configured to store a speaker model. The receiver is configured to receive an input audio signal. The processor is configured to determine a first confidence level associated with a first portion of the input audio signal based on the speaker model. The processor is also configured to determine a second confidence level associated with a second portion of the input audio signal based on the speaker model. The display is configured to present a graphical user interface associated with the first confidence level or associated with the second confidence level.
claims:
Claims QUALCOMM Ref. No. 151785WO CLAIMS: 1. A device comprising: a memory configured to store a speaker model; a receiver configured to receive an input audio signal; a processor configured to: determine a first confidence level associated with a first portion of the input audio signal based on the speaker model, and determine a second confidence level associated with a second portion of the input audio signal based on the speaker model; and a display configured to: present a graphical user interface associated with the first confidence level or associated with the second confidence level. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to update the speaker model based on the first portion of the input audio signal, wherein the second confidence level is determined based on the updated speaker model. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the processor is further configured to extract features of the first portion of the input audio signal, wherein the speaker model is updated by adding the features of the first portion of the input audio signal to model data of the speaker model. 4. The device of claim 1 wherein the second confidence level is higher than the first confidence level. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to determine a plurality of directions of arrival associated with the input audio signal and that the plurality of directions of arrival includes a first direction of arrival, wherein a first audio signal is extracted from the input audio signal based on the first direction of arrival, and wherein the first confidence level and the second confidence level are determined based on the first audio signal. 

 QUALCOMM Ref. No. 151785WO - 121 - 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the memory is configured to store a plurality of speaker models including the speaker model. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to determine a plurality of confidence levels associated with the first portion of the first audio signal based on the plurality of speaker models, determine that the first confidence level is a highest confidence level of the plurality of confidence levels, and select the speaker model. 8. The device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to extract a second audio signal from the input audio signal and determine a first particular confidence level associated with the second audio signal based on a second speaker model. 9. The device of claim 8, wherein the processor is further configured to: extract a first audio signal from the input audio signal; update the speaker model based on the first audio signal; and update the second speaker model based on the second audio signal. 10. A device comprising: a processor configured to: determine whether an input audio signal satisfies a speaker verification validation criterion; and predict a success rate associated with speaker verification based at least in part on one or more characteristics of the input audio signal; and a display configured to, in response to a determination that the input audio signal fails to satisfy the validation criterion, present a graphical user interface that indicates the predicted success rate. 

 QUALCOMM Ref. No. 151785WO - 122 - 11. The device of claim 10, wherein the predicted success rate is further based on a correlation between the one or more characteristics and historical confidence levels, wherein the speaker verification is determined to be successful based at least in part on a particular confidence level threshold, and wherein the predicted success rate is determined based on whether the historical confidence levels satisfy the particular confidence level threshold. 12. The device of claim 10, wherein the processor is further configured to, in response to determining that the input audio signal fails to satisfy the validation criterion, determine a suggested action to be performed by a user, and wherein the graphical user interface indicates the suggested action. 13. The device of claim 12, wherein the processor is further configured to determine a predicted change in the predicted success rate based on the user performing the suggested action during the speaker verification, and wherein the graphical user interface indicates the predicted change. 14. The device of claim 12, wherein the suggested action includes at least one of moving to a quieter location, moving closer to a microphone of the device, reducing user background noise, speaking slower, speaking in a neutral tone, speaking at a lower volume, speaking at a higher volume, speaking in a different language, speaking a longer phrase, or enunciating more distinctly. 15. The device of claim 10, wherein the graphical user interface further indicates that modifying a value of a parameter of the speaker verification changes the predicted success rate, and wherein the parameter includes at least one of a security level or a confidence level threshold. 16. The device of claim 10, wherein the processor is further configured to determine a confidence level associated with the input audio signal based on a speaker model, and wherein the graphical user interface further indicates the confidence level. 

 QUALCOMM Ref. No. 151785WO - 123 - 17. An apparatus comprising: means for determining whether an input audio signal satisfies a speaker verification validation criterion; means for predicting, in response to determining that the input audio signal fails to satisfy the validation criterion, a success rate associated with the speaker verification based at least in part on one or more characteristics of the input audio signal; and means for presenting a graphical user interface that indicates the predicted success rate. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the means for determining, the means for predicting, and the means for presenting are integrated into a communications device, a personal digital assistant (PDA), a computer, a music player, a video player, an entertainment unit, a navigation device, a fixed location data unit, or a set top box. 19. A method comprising: receiving an input audio signal at a device; determining a plurality of directions of arrival associated with the input audio signal and that the plurality of directions of arrival includes a first direction of arrival; extracting a first audio signal from the input audio signal based on the first direction of arrival; determining that the first audio signal satisfies one or more classification criteria; determining a reverberation time corresponding to the first audio signal; and selecting a speaker model from a plurality of speaker models based at least in part on the reverberation time. 

 QUALCOMM Ref. No. 151785WO - 124 - 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising: extracting features of the first audio signal; and updating the speaker model based on the first audio signal by adding the features to model data of the speaker model. 21. The method of claim 19, wherein the first direction of arrival corresponds to a single dominant direction of arrival, and wherein the first audio signal corresponds to a dominant audio signal. 22. The method of claim 19, wherein the one or more classification criteria indicate whether the first audio signal corresponds to non-standard speech, whether an amount of model data satisfies a model data threshold, or whether a confidence level of the first audio signal satisfies a confidence level threshold. 23. The method of claim 19, wherein the input audio signal is received during a voice call. 24. The method of claim 19, further comprising: determining that the plurality of directions of arrival includes a second direction of arrival; extracting a second audio signal from the input audio signal based on the second direction of arrival; determining that the second audio signal satisfies the one or more classification criteria; determining a second reverberation time corresponding to the second audio signal; selecting a second speaker model from the plurality of speaker models based at least in part on the second reverberation time; and updating the second speaker model based on the second audio signal. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the speaker model is associated with a first user, and wherein the second speaker model is associated with a second user. 

 QUALCOMM Ref. No. 151785WO - 125 - 26. The method of claim 24, further comprising: determining a first confidence level associated with the first audio signal based on the speaker model; determining a second confidence level associated with the second audio signal based on the second speaker model; and generating a graphical user interface that indicates the first confidence level and the second confidence level. 27. The method of claim 19, further comprising determining a plurality of confidence levels associated with the first audio signal based on the plurality of speaker models, wherein a first confidence level associated with the first audio signal is determined based on the speaker model, and wherein the speaker model is selected based on determining that the first confidence level indicates a highest confidence level of the plurality of confidence levels. 28. The method of claim 27, further comprising generating a graphical user interface that indicates the first confidence level. 29. The method of claim 27, further comprising generating a graphical user interface that indicates whether the first confidence level satisfies each of a plurality of confidence level thresholds associated with a plurality of applications. 30. The method of claim 19, further comprising: determining a noise level associated with the first audio signal; and determining a signal-to-noise ratio associated with the first audio signal, wherein the speaker model is selected further based on determining that the speaker model is associated with the signal-to-noise ratio, the noise level, or both.
